frameworks.name: Bayesian belief updating
frameworks.one_line: Reward predictions conditioned on probabilistic beliefs about hidden environmental state

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning (reward prediction error)
frameworks.one_line: RPE framework extended so predictions depend on inferred hidden-state beliefs

frameworks.name: non-Bayesian cognitive models
frameworks.one_line: Alternative learning models without explicit posterior over hidden states

core_question: Does nucleus accumbens dopamine release reflect Bayesian inference over hidden states during an instrumental two-armed bandit task?
key_assumptions: Dopamine release encodes a reward prediction error signal
Animals maintain probabilistic beliefs over the latent state of the environment
Reward predictions are formed conditional on those beliefs
manipulations: Stochastic switching of hidden state in a two-armed bandit task
Comparison across a wide family of cognitive learning models
key_findings: Behavioral model comparison favored models using Bayesian updating of hidden-state beliefs
Bayesian-belief models quantitatively matched mesolimbic dLight dopamine signals better than non-Bayesian alternatives
interpretations: Nucleus accumbens dopamine implicitly reflects probabilistic beliefs about the latent state
Bayesian inference over hidden states underlies reward prediction during instrumental learning
